name: org.machanism.machai:genai-client
description:
GenAI Client (org.machanism.machai:genai-client) is a Java library that provides a provider-agnostic client integration layer for Generative AI / LLM backends within the MachAI/Machanism ecosystem. It defines a common SPI (service-provider interface) for AI providers (org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider) supporting: accumulating system instructions and user prompts for a conversational run; attaching local files (java.io.File) or remote resources (java.net.URL) as model inputs; computing embedding vectors (List<Double>) for semantic use cases; registering host-executed tools/functions that a model can call during execution; executing a run to obtain a response string; and returning token usage metrics (Usage) for accounting.

Provider resolution is handled by org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProviderManager, which instantiates providers by reflection based on a model identifier formatted as "Provider:Model" (e.g., "OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini"). If the provider prefix is omitted, a default provider is used (NoneProvider). Providers can be referenced by short name (mapped to org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.<provider>.<Provider>Provider) or by fully-qualified class name.

Built-in provider integrations include:
- OpenAIProvider: functional implementation using the OpenAI Java SDK Responses API. Supports prompts, system instructions, file inputs (upload or URL), embeddings, tool/function calling, and usage reporting/aggregation.
- CodeMieProvider: integration with EPAM CodeMie that obtains an OAuth2 access token via an OIDC token endpoint (password grant or client_credentials) and then delegates to OpenAI-compatible / Gemini / Claude providers based on chatModel prefix.
- NoneProvider: no-op/offline provider that accumulates prompts and can write inputs/instructions to local files; perform() returns null.
- GeminiProvider and ClaudeProvider: currently placeholders/not fully implemented.

Host-integrated tool/function surface (org.machanism.machai.ai.tools) is provided via a FunctionTools SPI and discovered through ServiceLoader by FunctionToolsLoader. Tool sets register named functions into a GenAIProvider via addTool(name, description, ToolFunction, paramsDesc...). Included tool sets:
- FileFunctionTools: read/write/list files and recursive listing, relative to a working directory.
- WebFunctionTools: fetch web content (GET) and execute REST API calls with optional headers/body, supports basic auth via URL userInfo and ${...} placeholder substitution in header values via Configurator.
- CommandFunctionTools: run command line tool with deny-list security checks, working-directory confinement within project root, bounded output capture (LimitedStringBuilder), and a terminate_process tool to abort execution.

Typical usage: resolve a provider with GenAIProviderManager.getProvider("OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini", conf); configure provider.instructions(...); provider.prompt(...); optionally provider.addFile(...) and provider.addTool(...)/FunctionToolsLoader.applyTools(provider); call provider.perform(); then read provider.usage() and/or aggregate via GenAIProviderManager.logUsage().

Configuration keys used by providers include: chatModel; OPENAI_API_KEY; OPENAI_BASE_URL; GENAI_TIMEOUT; MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS; MAX_TOOL_CALLS; CodeMie-specific GENAI_USERNAME, GENAI_PASSWORD, and optional AUTH_URL; and tool-specific configuration such as ft.command.denylist for extending command deny rules.

constructors.description: Resolves and instantiates a GenAIProvider based on a provider/model identifier. The identifier is typically formatted as "Provider:Model" (e.g., "OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini"). If the provider part is omitted, the default provider is used. Providers are created via reflection (public no-arg constructor), the Configurator is updated with chatModel (model-only part), and provider.init(conf) is called.

examples.description: Creates and uses an OpenAI-backed provider instance, runs a prompt, and reads back token usage.

constructors.description: Returns a singleton loader that discovers FunctionTools implementations via java.util.ServiceLoader and can apply them to a GenAIProvider. The loader can also distribute a Configurator to tools for runtime placeholder substitution (e.g., ${...} in web headers or command strings).

examples.description: Installs all host-integrated tools (file, web, command) into a provider instance.

features.name: GenAIProvider.perform
features.description: Executes the provider using accumulated prompts, instructions, attached files, and registered tools to produce a response. Concrete providers may perform multiple iterations when tool calls are involved (e.g., OpenAIProvider executes tool calls, appends tool outputs, and re-queries until a final response is produced).

examples.description: Runs a single model invocation using prompts and system instructions.

features.name: GenAIProvider.addTool
features.signature: void org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider.addTool(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String description, org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider.ToolFunction function, java.lang.String... paramsDesc)
features.description: Registers a host-executed tool/function that a provider may expose to the model for function calling. ToolFunction receives an Object[] of provider-specific arguments and returns an Object result. In OpenAIProvider, the tool handler receives (0) a com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JsonNode of parsed arguments and (1) a java.io.File working directory; the provider dispatches by tool name.

examples.description: Registers a simple tool that reads a JSON argument and returns a string result.

features.name: GenAIProvider.embedding
features.description: Computes an embedding vector for the input text using a provider-specific embedding model and dimensionality. Implemented by OpenAIProvider; unsupported by NoneProvider (throws UnsupportedOperationException).

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider provider = /* OpenAIProvider */;
java.util.List<java.lang.Double> vec = provider.embedding("hello world", 1536L);
examples.description: Computes an embedding vector for downstream semantic search or similarity matching.

features.name: GenAIProviderManager.logUsage
features.description: Aggregates token usage records across runs and logs a summary of input, cached input, and output tokens. Usage aggregation is stored in a static list and is not synchronized.

examples.description: Logs total token usage across multiple provider invocations.

features.name: FileFunctionTools.applyTools
features.description: Installs file-system tools into a provider: read_file_from_file_system, write_file_to_file_system, list_files_in_directory, and get_recursive_file_list. All paths are interpreted relative to the working directory provided at tool invocation time.

examples.description: Registers file tools directly without ServiceLoader discovery.

features.name: WebFunctionTools.getWebContent
features.description: Host-side tool implementation for get_web_content: performs an HTTP GET to a URL, applies optional headers, timeout, charset decoding, optional CSS selector extraction, and optional HTML-to-text rendering. Supports basic authentication via URL userInfo converted to an Authorization header and supports ${...} placeholder substitution in URLs/headers when a Configurator is supplied.

examples.description: Registers web tools so the provider can expose them to function-calling capable models.

customizations.name: GenAIProvider implementations
customizations.description: Add a new provider by implementing org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider with a public no-arg constructor. It can then be resolved by GenAIProviderManager either via a short name (org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.<lowercase>.<Name>Provider) or a fully-qualified class name in the provider portion of the model identifier.

examples.description: Skeleton of a new provider implementation.

customizations.name: FunctionTools SPI
customizations.description: Extend host-executed tool surface by implementing org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools and registering it via ServiceLoader (META-INF/services/org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools). The tool installer can register one or more functions via GenAIProvider.addTool.

examples.description: Adds a new host-side function tool installer.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider
studs.description: Provider contract for integrating a concrete GenAI backend. Implementations collect prompts/instructions, attachments, and tools, execute perform() to produce a response, and report per-run token usage.

examples.description: Uses the GenAIProvider contract without binding to a specific vendor implementation.

methods: void addTool(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String description, org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider.ToolFunction function, java.lang.String... paramsDesc)

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools
studs.description: SPI for installing host-side function tools into a GenAIProvider, typically discovered via ServiceLoader and applied through FunctionToolsLoader. Includes helper support for resolving ${...} placeholders using a Configurator.

examples.description: Discovers and applies all tool installers to a provider.

examples.title: Resolve provider and run a prompt
Enable host tools for function calling
